properties.name: Gateway of India
properties.description: Iconic arch monument built during the British Raj, overlooking Mumbai Harbour.

properties.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus
properties.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and historic railway terminus blending Victorian Gothic and Indian architecture.

properties.name: Elephanta Caves
properties.description: UNESCO-listed cave temples on Elephanta Island, dedicated to Lord Shiva, dating to the 5th–8th centuries.

properties.name: Haji Ali Dargah
properties.description: Mosque and dargah built on an islet off Worli coast, accessible via a narrow causeway at low tide.

properties.name: Dhobi Ghat
properties.description: World's largest open-air laundry, where thousands of washermen have worked for over a century.

properties.name: Crawford Market
properties.description: Historic wholesale market for fruits, vegetables, and spices in a Norman-Gothic style building.

properties.name: Dharavi
properties.description: One of Asia's largest informal settlements, known for its thriving small industries including leather and pottery.

properties.name: Linking Road
properties.description: Bandra's famous shopping street lined with street stalls and branded stores selling clothes and accessories.

properties.name: Fashion Street
properties.description: Budget street market near Azad Maidan selling trendy clothes, footwear, and accessories at low prices.

properties.name: Chor Bazaar
properties.description: Mumbai's famous antique market, also known as Thieves Market, selling vintage goods and curiosities.

properties.name: Sanjay Gandhi National Park
properties.description: Large protected forest within city limits, home to leopards, ancient Buddhist caves, and the Kanheri Caves.

properties.name: Mahim Nature Park
properties.description: Urban biodiversity hotspot on reclaimed land beside the Mahim Creek, with mangrove walks and bird watching.

properties.name: Aarey Colony
properties.description: Verdant green zone on the outskirts of Mumbai, part of which lies within SGNP's buffer area.

properties.name: Juhu Beach
properties.description: Popular suburban beach famous for its evening food stalls, pav bhaji, and panoramic sunset views.

properties.name: Marine Drive
properties.description: C-shaped seafront promenade along the Arabian Sea, nicknamed the Queen's Necklace for its arc of street lights.

properties.name: Versova Beach
properties.description: Fishing village beach known for its citizen-led cleanup effort and vibrant coastal community.

properties.name: Aksa Beach
properties.description: Quiet, less-crowded beach in Malad West, popular for weekend picnics and water sports.

properties.name: Siddhivinayak Temple
properties.description: Revered Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Ganesha, one of the richest and most visited temples in Mumbai.

properties.name: Mahalakshmi Temple
properties.description: Coastal temple dedicated to Mahalakshmi, Mahakali, and Mahasaraswati, perched above the Arabian Sea.

properties.name: Mumba Devi Temple
properties.description: Ancient temple of the city's patron goddess Mumba Devi, after whom Mumbai is named.
